stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Deep ploughing of the land
Puddling and leveling the field
Incorporate well-decomposed farmyard manure

stages.title: Seed Preparation & Nursery
substeps: Select high-quality certified Basmati seeds
Treat seeds with fungicide (e.g., Carbendazim)
Soak and incubate seeds
Prepare wet nursery bed and sow

stages.title: Transplantation
substeps: Uproot seedlings carefully at 21 days
Maintain wider spacing (typically 20x15 cm) for tillering
Maintain a shallow water layer

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal dose of NPK
Apply top dressing of Urea in splits
Apply Zinc sulfate to prevent Khaira disease

stages.title: Water Management & Vegetative Growth
substeps: Maintain 2-5 cm water level during tillering
Drain water briefly before panicle initiation
Keep soil moist during grain filling

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor for yellow stem borer and leaf folder
Check for neck blast and bacterial leaf blight
Apply target-specific pesticides as needed

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Drain field 10 days before harvest
Harvest when panicles turn golden yellow
Thresh carefully to avoid grain breakage

stages.title: Post Harvest & Storage

Store in hermetic bags to preserve aroma
Grade and prepare for premium market selling

Add farmyard manure

Seed treatment with fungicide

Monitor rainfall

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough land 2-3 times to achieve fine tilth
Remove weeds and stubble
Apply organic manure and mix well

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Treat seeds with bio-fungicides (Trichoderma)
Sow seeds using seed drill or broadcasting
Maintain depth of 2-3 cm

stages.title: Thinning & Gap Filling
substeps: Remove weak seedlings at 15 days
Fill gaps to maintain optimal plant population
Maintain intra-row spacing of 10-15 cm

stages.title: Weeding & Hoeing
substeps: Perform first inter-cultivation at 20 days
Hand weed to keep field clean
Apply pre-emergence herbicides if required

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal dose of Nitrogen and Phosphorus
Top dress with remaining Nitrogen at tillering stage
Monitor for micronutrient deficiency

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor for shoot fly and stem borer
Check for downy mildew and ergot diseases
Uproot and destroy infected plants

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Check if earheads are dry and grains are hard
Cut the earheads first (or cut entire plant)
Sun dry the harvested earheads

stages.title: Threshing & Storage
substeps: Thresh using mechanical thresher or manually
Winnow to separate grains from chaff
Dry to 10-12% moisture and store safely

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Deep plough followed by harrowing
Level field to ensure even moisture distribution
Pre-sowing irrigation (rauni) if soil is dry

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Select smut-resistant varieties
Treat seeds with Vitavax or Bavistin
Sow using seed drill at 22.5 cm row spacing

stages.title: Crown Root Initiation & Irrigation
substeps: Apply first crucial irrigation at CRI stage (25-30 days)
Monitor soil moisture levels
Ensure no waterlogging occurs

stages.title: Fertilizer Application & Weeding
substeps: Apply half Nitrogen and full Phosphorus at sowing
Top dress remaining Nitrogen after first irrigation
Apply broadleaf herbicides if weed density is high

stages.title: Tillering & Booting Stage
substeps: Apply second irrigation during active tillering
Monitor for nutrient deficiencies
Apply third irrigation at booting/heading stage

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor for aphids and rusts (yellow/brown)
Check for loose smut and covered smut
Spray appropriate fungicides/insecticides

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when moisture is around 18-20%
Cut crop close to the ground
Allow crop to dry in the field for a few days

stages.title: Threshing, Grading & Storage
substeps: Thresh the dried crop
Grade grains based on plumpness (crucial for malting)
Store at 10-12% moisture in fumigated bins

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough field once or twice during summer
Harrow to create a fine, firm seedbed
Incorporate well-rotted FYM

stages.title: Seed Selection & Sowing
substeps: Treat seed with Azospirillum
Sow in rows with 25-30 cm spacing
Ensure sowing depth does not exceed 2-3 cm

stages.title: Thinning & Weeding
substeps: Thin plants to maintain 8-10 cm intra-row spacing at 15 days
Perform first hand weeding at 20 days
Perform inter-cultivation to manage soil aeration

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal dose of NPK
Top dress with remaining Nitrogen at 30 days
Use bio-fertilizers for better yield

stages.title: Irrigation & Maintenance
substeps: Rely primarily on rainfall (rainfed crop)
Provide life-saving irrigation during tillering if dry spell occurs
Ensure field is well-drained

stages.title: Pest & Disease Management
substeps: Monitor for shoot fly and rust diseases
Check for grain smut
Apply botanical pesticides (e.g., Neem oil) if needed

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when panicles turn yellowish-brown
Cut earheads or harvest whole plants
Dry harvested material in the sun

stages.title: Post Harvest & Processing
substeps: Thresh using stone rollers or mechanical threshers
Dehusk the grains (processing is crucial for millets)
Store dried grains in gunny bags

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Deep summer ploughing to expose pests
Harrowing to break clods and level the soil
Apply farmyard manure and incorporate it well

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Treat seeds with Thiram or Captan
Sow seeds at a depth of 3-4 cm
Maintain row spacing of 45 cm

stages.title: Thinning & Weeding
substeps: Thin out weak seedlings 15 days after sowing
Maintain plant-to-plant spacing of 15 cm
Perform inter-cultivation to control weeds (especially Striga)

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal dose of NPK at sowing
Top dress with Nitrogen at 30 days
Apply micronutrients if soil is deficient

stages.title: Water Management & Vegetative Growth
substeps: Provide critical irrigation at seedling and boot stages
Ensure proper drainage to prevent waterlogging
Monitor drought stress during flowering

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor for shoot fly and stem borer
Check for grain smut and rust
Apply recommended insecticides/fungicides

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when grains become hard and have less than 20% moisture
Cut the earheads separately or harvest the whole stalk
Sun dry the harvested crop

stages.title: Post Harvest & Storage
substeps: Thresh the earheads to separate grains
Clean and dry grains to 10-12% moisture
Store in pest-proof bins or gunny bags

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the field 2-3 times to get a fine tilth
Remove previous crop debris
Apply compost or well-rotted FYM

stages.title: Seed Treatment
substeps: Select healthy, disease-free seeds
Treat seeds with Agrosan or bio-fertilizers like Azospirillum
Dry treated seeds in shade

stages.title: Sowing & Thinning
substeps: Line sow or broadcast seeds uniformly
Maintain row spacing of 22-25 cm
Thin out excess seedlings 15 days after emergence

stages.title: Weeding
substeps: First hand weeding at 20-25 days after sowing
Second weeding at 40 days if weed pressure is high
Use a hoe for inter-cultivation

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal dose of Nitrogen and Phosphorus
Avoid excessive Nitrogen to prevent lodging
Top dress with remaining Nitrogen at tillering

stages.title: Growth & Irrigation
substeps: Rely mainly on rainfall (highly drought-tolerant)
Provide life-saving irrigation during prolonged dry spells
Ensure no standing water in the field

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor for shoot fly and head bug
Check for head smut disease
Remove and destroy infected plants

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when panicles turn light brown and grains are hard
Cut the crop close to the ground
Leave in the field for 2-3 days for sun drying

stages.title: Post Harvest
substeps: Thresh under a stone roller, tractor tread, or mechanical thresher
Dehusk the grains carefully
Store dried grains in a cool, dry place

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Deep plough the field to 20-25 cm depth
Prepare ridges and furrows or a flat seedbed
Apply FYM evenly across the field

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Treat seeds with fungicides (e.g., Thiram)
Sow seeds on the sides of ridges (dibbling method)
Maintain plant spacing of 60x20 cm

stages.title: Weeding & Earthing Up
substeps: Apply pre-emergence herbicide like Atrazine
Perform hand weeding 20 days after sowing
Earth up the base of plants at 30 days to provide support

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal NPK and Zinc Sulphate
Top dress with Urea at knee-high stage (V8 stage)
Apply final Nitrogen dose at tasseling

stages.title: Irrigation & Tasseling/Silking
substeps: Irrigate immediately after sowing
Ensure strict moisture availability during tasseling and silking
Avoid waterlogging during early growth

stages.title: Pest & Disease Management
substeps: Scout vigorously for Fall Armyworm and stem borer
Apply recommended pesticides (e.g., Spinetoram) if threshold is crossed
Monitor for leaf blight and stalk rot

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Check for black layer formation at the base of the kernel
Harvest cobs when husks turn dry and brown
Sun dry the harvested cobs for 3-4 days

stages.title: Post Harvest & Storage
substeps: Shell the grains from the cobs mechanically or manually
Dry grains to 12-14% moisture
Fumigate and store in silos or airtight bags

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the main field 2-3 times
Level the land to conserve moisture
Incorporate organic manure thoroughly

stages.title: Nursery Preparation & Sowing
substeps: Prepare raised nursery beds
Treat seeds with Trichoderma viride
Sow seeds thinly in lines and cover with soil/compost
Water the nursery regularly

stages.title: Transplanting & Thinning
substeps: Pull out 21-25 day old robust seedlings
Transplant in the main field with 25x15 cm spacing
If direct sown, thin out excess seedlings

stages.title: Weeding
substeps: First hand weeding 15-20 days after transplanting
Use a wheel hoe for inter-cultivation
Second weeding at 35-40 days

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal NPK formulation
Top dress with Nitrogen 30 days after transplanting
Ensure even distribution of fertilizers

stages.title: Irrigation & Growth
substeps: Irrigate the field at the time of transplanting
Provide life-saving irrigation at tillering and flowering stages
Monitor rainfall to adjust irrigation schedules

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor strictly for Blast disease (leaf, neck, and finger blast)
Spray Tricyclazole if blast symptoms appear
Check for stem borer and aphids

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when earheads turn brown and grains are hard
Cut the earheads with sickles
Leave earheads on the threshing floor to dry

stages.title: Post Harvest
substeps: Thresh by beating with sticks or using a thresher
Winnow to clean the grains
Dry well to 10-12% moisture for long-term storage

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Perform shallow ploughing in summer
Harrow to create a fine, firm seedbed
Clear field of previous crop roots and weeds

stages.title: Seed Preparation
substeps: Treat seeds with bio-fungicides or Agrosan
Ensure seeds are well dried before sowing
Mix seeds with sand if broadcasting to ensure even spread

stages.title: Sowing & Thinning
substeps: Sow in rows 25-30 cm apart using a seed drill
Sow at a shallow depth of 2-3 cm
Thin out weak plants at 15 days to maintain spacing

stages.title: Weeding
substeps: Keep the field weed-free for the first 30 days
Perform first inter-cultivation 20 days after sowing
Hand weed as necessary

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal NPK according to soil test recommendations
Top dress with Urea at the vegetative stage
Use compost to improve soil moisture retention

stages.title: Growth & Irrigation
substeps: Primarily cultivated as a rainfed crop
Irrigate only during prolonged dry spells at critical stages (tillering, grain filling)
Prevent water stagnation at all costs

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor for armyworms and shoot fly
Check for rust and smut diseases
Apply appropriate organic or chemical controls

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when panicles turn yellowish-brown
Cut the entire plant or just the earheads
Dry the harvested crop in the sun for 2-3 days

stages.title: Post Harvest
substeps: Thresh using conventional methods or mechanical threshers
Dehusk processing is required before consumption
Store the grains securely away from moisture

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the field using a disc or mouldboard plough
Harrow and level the field to ensure uniform irrigation
Apply pre-sowing irrigation (rauni) for optimal moisture

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Treat seeds with Carboxin or Tebuconazole to prevent loose smut
Sow seeds using a seed drill at a depth of 5 cm
Maintain row-to-row spacing of 20-22.5 cm

stages.title: Crown Root Initiation (CRI) & Weeding
substeps: Apply first crucial irrigation at 21 days (CRI stage)
Apply post-emergence broadleaf herbicides if needed
Perform hand weeding to reduce early competition

stages.title: Tillering & Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply second irrigation at late tillering stage
Top dress with Urea to support vigorous vegetative growth
Monitor plant population and tillering density

stages.title: Jointing & Booting
substeps: Apply third irrigation at the late jointing/booting stage
Monitor for symptoms of yellow or brown rust
Apply protective fungicides if disease pressure is high

stages.title: Flowering & Milking
substeps: Provide mild irrigation to prevent moisture stress during flowering
Monitor for aphid infestations on the earheads
Ensure crop is protected from sudden high temperatures (terminal heat)

stages.title: Dough Stage & Maturity
substeps: Stop irrigation to allow grains to harden
Leaves turn yellow and plant starts drying
Check grain moisture content regularly

stages.title: Harvesting & Post Harvest
substeps: Harvest using combine harvesters or manually with sickles
Sun dry the grains to bring moisture down to 10-12%
Store in gunny bags or silos treated with safe fumigants

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Deep summer ploughing to eradicate deep-rooted weeds
Apply well-decomposed farmyard manure or compost
Prepare ridges and furrows for proper drainage

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Treat seeds with Rhizobium culture and Trichoderma viride
Dibble seeds on ridges with 60-90 cm row spacing
Maintain plant-to-plant spacing of 20-30 cm

stages.title: Early Vegetative Growth & Weeding
substeps: Keep field weed-free for the first 45 days
Perform first hand weeding 25-30 days after sowing
Apply pre-emergence herbicides like Pendimethalin

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal dose of Phosphorus and Sulphur
Apply Zinc Sulphate if soil is deficient
Ensure root nodulation is progressing well

stages.title: Branching & Irrigation
substeps: Provide life-saving irrigation during long dry spells
Ensure field is not waterlogged (highly sensitive to stagnation)
Perform nipping of apical buds to encourage lateral branching

stages.title: Flowering & Pod Formation
substeps: Provide critical irrigation at flowering stage
Monitor strictly for Gram Pod Borer (Helicoverpa armigera)
Install pheromone traps and spray appropriate insecticides

stages.title: Pod Development & Maturity
substeps: Check for pod fly and blister beetle damage
Observe leaves dropping and pods turning brown/dry
Stop irrigation completely

stages.title: Harvesting & Post Harvest
substeps: Cut plants from the base with sickles when 80% pods are mature
Sun dry the harvested plants on the threshing floor for a week
Thresh manually or mechanically, clean, and store at 10% moisture

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the land 1-2 times to achieve fine tilth
Level the field to avoid water stagnation
Apply basal dose of FYM

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Treat seeds with Rhizobium culture
Sow in rows with 30-45 cm spacing
Sow at a depth of 3-4 cm

stages.title: Weeding & Early Growth
substeps: Perform first hand weeding 20 days after sowing
Use a hoe for inter-cultivation to aerate the soil
Maintain optimal plant population

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal NPK specifically formulated for pulses
Use foliar spray of 2% DAP if crop shows poor growth
Ensure adequate Phosphorus for root nodulation

stages.title: Irrigation & Branching
substeps: Irrigate before flowering if soil moisture is low
Ensure excellent drainage, as crop is sensitive to waterlogging
Monitor for early pest attacks

stages.title: Flowering & Pod Formation
substeps: Monitor for aphids, leafhoppers, and pod borers
Spray Neem seed kernel extract (NSKE) or appropriate pesticides
Ensure adequate moisture for grain filling

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Pick mature, dry pods periodically (multiple pickings)
Alternatively, harvest the whole plant when most pods turn yellow-brown
Avoid delayed harvesting to prevent pod shattering

stages.title: Post Harvest
substeps: Sun dry the harvested pods for 3-4 days
Beat gently with sticks to extract the grains
Clean, dry to 9-10% moisture, and store with dry neem leaves

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the land thoroughly to a good depth
Harrow to break clods and create a flat seedbed
Prepare ridges if sowing in a high-rainfall area

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Select healthy seeds and treat with appropriate bio-fungicides
Sow seeds by dibbling method at 45x15 cm spacing
Ensure adequate soil moisture during sowing for germination

stages.title: Vegetative Growth & Weeding
substeps: Keep the field weed-free during the initial 30 days
Perform hand weeding or shallow inter-cultivation
Perform earthing up to provide support to the stem

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply mainly Phosphorus and Potassium as basal dose
Apply minimal starter Nitrogen to boost initial growth
Utilize organic compost to improve soil texture

stages.title: Flowering & Irrigation
substeps: Provide irrigation at pre-flowering and flowering stages
Monitor for flower drop caused by environmental stress
Apply planar growth regulators if flower shedding is excessive

stages.title: Pest Management
substeps: Monitor closely for pod borers (Maruca vitrata) and stink bugs
Apply botanical pesticides or recommended chemical sprays
Remove severely infected plant parts manually

stages.title: Pod Maturity & Harvesting
substeps: Harvest pods when they turn dry and papery
Conduct multiple pickings as pods mature unevenly
Collect pods in baskets and transport to drying area

stages.title: Post Harvest
substeps: Dry the pods thoroughly under the sun
Extract seeds by threshing or beating with sticks
Grade the seeds and store in moisture-proof containers

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Conserve soil moisture from the preceding monsoon season
Perform rough seedbed preparation (avoids fine tilth to improve aeration)
Clear stubbles from the previous kharif crop

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Treat seeds with Trichoderma to prevent wilt disease
Treat with Rhizobium culture specifically for chickpeas
Sow deep (8-10 cm) to ensure seeds reach the moisture zone

stages.title: Early Growth & Nipping
substeps: Monitor for early signs of collar rot or wilt
Perform nipping (plucking of apical buds) at 30-35 days
Nipping promotes extensive lateral branching and more flowers

stages.title: Weeding
substeps: First hand weeding at 25-30 days post-sowing
Apply pre-emergence herbicide like Pendimethalin if hand weeding is difficult
Maintain proper aeration around the root zone

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal dose of Phosphorus and Sulphur via drill
Bengal gram is highly responsive to Phosphorus
Ensure fertilizer is placed below the seed layer

stages.title: Flowering & Irrigation
substeps: Apply irrigation at the pre-flowering stage if soil moisture is severely depleted
Strictly avoid irrigation during full bloom to prevent flower drop
Apply second irrigation during the pod development stage if needed

stages.title: Pod Formation & Pest Control
substeps: Monitor rigorously for Gram Pod Borer (Helicoverpa armigera)
Set up bird perches and pheromone traps in the field
Apply HaNPV (virus spray) or chemical insecticides at economic threshold levels

stages.title: Harvesting & Post Harvest
substeps: Harvest when leaves turn reddish-brown to yellow and start shedding
Cut the plants close to the ground using sickles
Sun dry on the threshing floor, thresh mechanically, and store at <10% moisture

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the field to achieve a fine tilth
Ensure proper leveling for uniform irrigation
Apply well-decomposed farmyard manure

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Treat seeds with Rhizobium culture
Treat with Imidacloprid to protect against early sucking pests
Line sow at a depth of 3-4 cm with 30x10 cm spacing

stages.title: Weeding
substeps: Apply pre-emergence herbicide (e.g., Pendimethalin)
Perform hand weeding 20-25 days after sowing
Keep the field weed-free during the critical early growth stage

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal dose of Nitrogen and Phosphorus
Apply Sulphur if the soil is deficient
Foliar spray of 2% DAP at pre-flowering stage if growth is poor

stages.title: Irrigation & Growth
substeps: Provide life-saving irrigation during prolonged dry spells
Irrigate at critical stages: pre-flowering and pod formation
Ensure excellent drainage to prevent waterlogging

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor strictly for Whiteflies (vector for Yellow Mosaic Virus)
Uproot and destroy YMV infected plants immediately
Spray appropriate insecticides for pod borers

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when 80% of the pods turn black and dry
Cut the whole plant with sickles
Avoid delayed harvesting to prevent pod shattering

stages.title: Post Harvest & Storage
substeps: Sun dry the harvested crop for 3-4 days
Thresh manually or using a mechanical thresher
Dry grains to 9-10% moisture and store safely

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the land 1-2 times to loosen the soil
Prepare ridges and furrows for better drainage
Incorporate organic compost into the soil

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Treat seeds with Trichoderma viride
Sow seeds at a spacing of 45x15 cm
Ensure adequate soil moisture at the time of sowing

stages.title: Weeding & Hoeing
substeps: First hand weeding at 20 days after sowing
Hoe the field to aerate the root zone
Maintain a weed-free environment up to 35 days

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal dose of NPK
Ensure adequate Phosphorus for proper root nodulation
Apply micronutrients if symptoms of deficiency appear

stages.title: Irrigation & Branching
substeps: Irrigate only if the soil is excessively dry (highly drought-tolerant)
Provide irrigation during the flowering and pod development stages
Prevent water stagnation

stages.title: Pest & Disease Management
substeps: Monitor for aphids and leafhoppers
Check for symptoms of Cowpea Mosaic Virus
Apply Neem-based bio-pesticides or recommended chemicals

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: For vegetable purpose, pick tender green pods regularly
For grains, harvest when pods turn yellow/brown and dry
Conduct multiple pickings if maturity is uneven

stages.title: Post Harvest & Storage
substeps: Dry the mature pods in the sun
Thresh by beating with pliable bamboo sticks
Clean and store seeds with neem leaves to prevent pulse beetles

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Deep ploughing followed by 1-2 harrowings
Level the field to ensure no water logging
Ensure fine seedbed preparation for uniform germination

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Treat seeds with Rhizobium leguminosarum
Treat with fungicides (e.g., Thiram) to prevent soil-borne diseases
Sow using a seed drill with 30 cm row spacing

stages.title: Weeding & Early Growth
substeps: Apply pre-emergence herbicides to control early weeds
Perform one hand weeding at 30 days if required
Avoid disturbing the soil once flowering begins

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply starter dose of Nitrogen
Apply full dose of Phosphorus and Potash at sowing time
Place fertilizers in bands 5 cm away from seeds

stages.title: Irrigation Management
substeps: Apply first irrigation at the pre-flowering stage (45 days)
Apply second irrigation at the pod-filling stage
Ensure light irrigation as peas are highly sensitive to excess water

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor for powdery mildew, especially late in the season
Apply wettable sulphur if powdery mildew appears
Check for pea stem fly and aphids; spray Dimethoate if needed

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when plants turn yellow and pods are completely dry
Harvest early in the morning to prevent pod shattering
Cut the plants close to the ground

stages.title: Post Harvest & Storage
substeps: Dry the harvested crop in the field for a few days
Thresh using a mechanical thresher or under tractor wheels
Clean the grains, dry to 10% moisture, and store in cool conditions

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the land quickly after the previous crop harvest
Prepare a fine tilth by harrowing
Level the field to retain optimal moisture

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Treat seeds with appropriate Rhizobium culture
Sow in lines using seed drill at 30x10 cm spacing
Maintain a sowing depth of 3-4 cm

stages.title: Weeding
substeps: Apply pre-emergence herbicide like Pendimethalin
Hand weed at 20 days after sowing
Keep crop weed-free for the first 30 days

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal NPK as recommended by soil test
Foliar spray of 2% Urea at flowering if plants look pale
Ensure Sulphur is applied if cultivating in deficient soils

stages.title: Irrigation & Vegetative Growth
substeps: Provide life-saving irrigation at branching stage if dry
Ensure adequate moisture during flowering and pod development
Strictly prevent water stagnation

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor rigorously for Whiteflies and Yellow Mosaic Virus (YMV)
Check for thrips and pod borers during flowering
Apply recommended systemic insecticides if threshold is crossed

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when 85% of pods mature and turn black/brown
Pick pods manually in 2-3 intervals (if asynchronous maturity)
Or harvest whole plants if synchronized maturing variety is used

stages.title: Post Harvest & Storage
substeps: Sun dry the harvested pods/plants for 2-3 days
Thresh carefully to avoid splitting the delicate grains
Dry grains to 8-9% moisture and store in airtight containers

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Deep ploughing followed by harrowing
Create raised beds or ridges to avoid waterlogging
Incorporate well-rotted FYM uniformly

stages.title: Seed Sowing & Support
substeps: Select sweet, wrinkle-seeded varieties for green peas
Treat seeds with Captan or Thiram
Sow in rows; provide staking or trellis support for tall varieties

stages.title: Weeding & Hoeing
substeps: Perform shallow hoeing to control weeds and aerate soil
First weeding at 20-25 days after sowing
Avoid deep hoeing to protect the shallow root system

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal dose of NPK (high Phosphorus requirement)
Apply Molybdenum if soil pH is highly acidic
Top dress with Nitrogen just before flowering if needed

stages.title: Irrigation Management
substeps: Maintain steady soil moisture throughout the growth period
Irrigate immediately after sowing and again at pre-flowering
Ensure adequate moisture during pod swelling

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor for pea leaf miner and aphids
Check for powdery mildew and rust

stages.title: Harvesting

Cut the stalk below the curd leaving 2-3 jacket leaves for protection

stages.title: Post Harvest & Storage
substeps: Handle curds carefully to avoid bruising which causes browning
Sort and pack in plastic crates or baskets
Transport rapidly to market or cold store at 0°C and 95% RH

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the land 1-2 times to achieve a moderate tilth
Level the field to ensure uniform drainage
Apply a basal dose of farmyard manure

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Treat seeds with Rhizobium culture to enhance nitrogen fixation
Sow seeds in lines at a spacing of 45x15 cm
Sow at a depth of 2-3 cm

stages.title: Weeding & Hoeing
substeps: Perform first hand weeding 20-25 days after sowing
Use a wheel hoe for inter-cultivation to keep weeds in check
Maintain a weed-free field during early vegetative growth

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply a basal dose of Phosphorus and Potassium
Nitrogen requirement is low as it is a leguminous crop
Apply a light top dressing of Nitrogen if early growth is sluggish

stages.title: Irrigation & Branching
substeps: Highly drought-tolerant; irrigate only during prolonged dry spells
Provide life-saving irrigation at pre-flowering and pod formation stages
Strictly avoid waterlogging

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor for aphids, jassids, and whiteflies
Check for powdery mildew and bacterial leaf blight
Apply wettable sulphur or appropriate fungicides/insecticides

stages.title: Pod Development & Harvesting
substeps: For vegetable purpose, harvest tender, green pods periodically
For guar gum/seed purpose, allow pods to fully mature and turn brown
Conduct multiple pickings for vegetable use to encourage more flowering

stages.title: Post Harvest
substeps: For vegetables, pack tender pods in gunny bags and dispatch immediately
For seeds, harvest the whole plant when mature, dry in sun, and thresh
Store cleaned seeds in a dry, pest-proof area

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the land deeply 3-4 times to loosen the soil thoroughly
Incorporate a heavy dose of well-rotted FYM or compost
Prepare ridges and furrows at a spacing of 60 cm

stages.title: Seed Corm Preparation & Planting
substeps: Select healthy, disease-free medium-sized corms or cormels
Treat corms with cow dung slurry and Trichoderma viride
Plant corms at a depth of 5-7 cm on the ridges at 45 cm spacing

stages.title: Early Sprouting & Weeding
substeps: Mulch the field with dry leaves or paddy straw to conserve moisture and suppress weeds
Sprouting takes 2-3 weeks; maintain optimal soil moisture
Perform manual weeding carefully to avoid disturbing sprouting corms

stages.title: Earthing Up & Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal NPK along with heavy organic manures
Top dress with Nitrogen at 45 and 60 days after planting
Perform critical earthing up operations to cover growing corms and promote lateral tuber expansion

stages.title: Irrigation Management
substeps: Colocasia requires constant moisture and is highly sensitive to drought
Irrigate every 4-5 days during dry periods
Maintain wet, but not completely stagnant, soil conditions

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor rigorously for Phytophthora leaf blight (especially in high humidity)
Spray Mancozeb or Copper Oxychloride at the first sign of blight
Check for aphids and taro caterpillars; manage manually or with bio-pesticides

stages.title: Tuber Maturation & Harvesting
substeps: Maturity is indicated by the yellowing and dying down of the leaves
Stop irrigation completely 10-15 days before harvest
Dig out the corms and cormels carefully using a spade without injuring them

stages.title: Post Harvest (Cleaning & Curing)
substeps: Remove roots and excessive soil from the corms
Separate the main corms from the side cormels
Dry them under shade for a few days (curing) to improve shelf life before marketing

substeps: Jaminichi 2-3 tinda nangarani kara
Shetat changle kujlele shenkhat misala
Sari-varambe kiva gaadiphaave tayar kara

stages.title: Biya Perani

Thiram kiva Carbendazim ne bijprakriya kara
Don biyanchyamadhye 30-45 cm antar rakha

stages.title: Velichi Vaadh va Nindani

Velila adhaar dya kiva mandapavar chadhva
Zadachya bhowati halki bhar dya

stages.title: Khat va Pani Vyavasthapan

Phulara yetana pani niyamit dya
Soil moisture maintain kara जेणेकरून phale tatanar nahit

stages.title: Rog va Kid Niyantran

stages.title: Kadhani

Sakalchya kiva sandhyakalchya veli kadhani kara
Phalanchi grading karun marketla pathva

Khadde unhat changle tapu dya

stages.title: Ropanchi Lagwad

Ropanchi lagwad khaddyachya madhyabhagi kara
Lagwadizalyavar lagech pani dya

stages.title: Chatani va Vaadh

Zadala ghumata-sarkha aakar dya

stages.title: Khat va Pani

Nitrogen va Potash cha hapta dya
Micro-nutrients chi favarni kara

stages.title: Phulara va Shenga Vaadh

Shenga lagnyasathi pani niyojit kara
Kid (Bud worm) pasun sanrakshan kara

stages.title: Kadhani

Jhelene kiva haatane haluwar kadha
Bunding karun marketla dispatch kara

Khadde kiva khola sari tayar kara
Heavy dose shenkhat misala

stages.title: Kandachi Lagwad

Gomutra kiva fungiside ne treatment kara
Kand jaminichya kholi-var lava

stages.title: Achhadan (Mulching)
substeps: Korda pala-pachola vaparun mulching kara
Soil moisture maintain karnya-sathi duba-lagwad kara
Tan-niyantranasathi madat hote

stages.title: Khat va Pani
substeps: Lagwadinantar 2-3 mahinyanni khat dya
Pavsalyat pani vyavasthapan monitor kara
Zadachya bhowati maati chi bhar dya

stages.title: Kandachi Vaadh
substeps: Paane pivli padu lagli ki vaadh thambate
Kand jaminikhali motha honyasathi vel dya
Kid (Aphids) pasun kaalji ghya

stages.title: Kadhani
substeps: Paane poornapane vaal-lyavar kadhani kara
Kandala zakham honar nahi yaachi kaalji ghya
Kand saaf karun korda jagi saatha

stages.title: Rop-vatika (Nursery)
substeps: Gaadiphavya-var biya perani kara
Ropanna niyamit pani dya
Bij-prakriya karnya-sathi Captan vapara

stages.title: Punarlagwad (Transplanting)
substeps: 4-5 paane asatana ropanchi lagwad kara
Don ropant 25-30 cm antar rakha
Lagwad sandhyakalchya veli kara

stages.title: Khat va Nindani
substeps: Apply basal NPK dose
Tan-mukta shet rakha
Gadda ban-nyasathi Nitrogen cha hapta dya

stages.title: Gadda Vaadh va Pani
substeps: Niyamit pani dya (Irrigation cycle)
Gadda jun kiva fibrous honar nahi yaachi kaalji ghya
Kid (Cabbage butterfly) monitor kara

stages.title: Kadhani
substeps: Gadda 5-8 cm diameter cha zalavar kadha
Haluwar kadhani karun paane trim kara
Marketla lagech dispatch kara

stages.title: Jaminichi Mashagat
substeps: Nangarani karun shet khula kara
Pits kiva sari tayar kara
Shenkhat va organic compost dya

stages.title: Kalamachi Lagwad (Cuttings)
substeps: Jun veliche 20-30 cm che kalam ghya
Kalam jaminit tirki lava
Lagwadivar pani dya

stages.title: Mandap Tayari
substeps: Velila var chadhavnyasathi taranchi jali kara
Bambu kiva cement pole vapara
Velila jute thread ne adhaar dya

stages.title: Velichi Vaadh va Khat
substeps: Urea va NPK splits madhye dya
Extra shoots trim kara जेणेकरून main vel vadhel
Pani niyojan thibakne kara

stages.title: Phulara va Phale
substeps: Phulara stage-var pani tanchai hou deu naka
Fruit fly pasun pheromone traps vapara
Boron spray karnya-sathi vichar kara

stages.title: Kadhani
substeps: Phale hirvi va kovli asatana kadhani kara
Niyamit 2-3 divasanni kadhani kara
Size nusar grading karun vikri kara

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the field 2-3 times to a fine tilth
Incorporate well-decomposed FYM (Farm Yard Manure)
Prepare long channels or circular pits

stages.title: Seed Sowing
substeps: Soak seeds in water for 12 hours for better germination
Treat seeds with bio-fungicides to prevent root rot
Sow 2-3 seeds per pit at a depth of 1.5-2 cm

stages.title: Vine Growth & Thinning
substeps: Thin out leaving the 1-2 healthiest vines per pit after 10 days
Perform shallow weeding to avoid disturbing the root system
Train vines on raised beds to keep fruits off moist soil

stages.title: Fertilizer & Irrigation
substeps: Apply top dressing of Nitrogen to boost vine growth
Irrigate frequently in summer (every 3-4 days)
Maintain even soil moisture to prevent bitter fruits

stages.title: Flowering & Fruit Set
substeps: Ensure adequate bee activity for pollination
Spray Neem oil if aphids or whiteflies are detected
Avoid pesticide application during morning hours to protect pollinators

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when fruits are slender, tender, and light green
Pick fruits regularly (every 2-3 days) to encourage more fruiting
Handle delicately to prevent bruising

stages.title: Nursery Preparation
substeps: Prepare raised nursery beds of 1-meter width
Treat seeds with Thiram and sow in lines 5-7 cm apart
Water daily using a fine rose can and cover with dry mulch until germination

stages.title: Land Prep & Transplanting
substeps: Deep plough the main field and prepare flat beds or broad bed furrows (BBF)
Uproot 45-day-old seedlings, dip roots in Azospirillum slurry
Transplant at a spacing of 15x10 cm and irrigate immediately

stages.title: Weeding & Fertilizer
substeps: Apply basal NPK and Sulphur (Sulphur is crucial for pungency and storage)
Apply pre-emergence herbicide like Oxyfluorfen
Perform first manual weeding 30 days after transplanting

stages.title: Vegetative Growth
substeps: Top dress with Urea 30 and 45 days after transplanting
Maintain consistent soil moisture with frequent, light irrigation
Monitor for thrips and purple blotch disease

stages.title: Bulb Initiation & Development
substeps: Stop Nitrogen application to prevent thick necks
Ensure no water stress occurs during bulb enlargement
Apply Potassium foliar spray to improve bulb size and quality

stages.title: Irrigation Withholding & Neck Fall
substeps: Stop irrigation completely 10-15 days before harvest
Wait for 50-60% of plant tops (necks) to fall over naturally
This indicates bulb maturity and improves storage life

stages.title: Harvesting & Curing
substeps: Pull the bulbs manually or use a bulb digger
Cure bulbs in the field by windrowing (covering bulbs with leaves of the next row) for 3-5 days
Cut the tops leaving 2 cm neck, sort, and store in well-ventilated structures (Chawl)

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the field to a medium tilth
Ensure excellent drainage, as peas are highly sensitive to waterlogging
Apply well-rotted FYM uniformly

stages.title: Seed Treatment & Sowing
substeps: Treat seeds with Rhizobium leguminosarum
Treat with Trichoderma to prevent wilt and root rot
Sow in rows at a spacing of 30x10 cm using a seed drill

stages.title: Early Growth & Weeding
substeps: Apply pre-emergence weedicide like Pendimethalin
Perform one hand hoeing 20-25 days after sowing
Erect stakes or small twigs for support if growing a tall/vining variety

stages.title: Flowering & Irrigation
substeps: Apply first crucial irrigation at the pre-flowering stage
Do not overwater; heavy irrigation causes yellowing and stunted growth
Apply foliar spray of micronutrients if leaves pale

stages.title: Pod Filling & Pest Control
substeps: Provide second light irrigation during the pod swelling stage
Monitor strictly for Powdery Mildew and spray wettable sulphur
Check for pea pod borer and aphids; apply appropriate control measures

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when green pods are well-filled, crisp, and bright green
Conduct 3-4 pickings at intervals of 7-10 days
Pick early in the morning to retain maximum sweetness and moisture

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Select well-drained sandy loam soil (crucial for Parval)
Perform deep ploughing and prepare trenches or pits
Incorporate heavy organic manure, neem cake, and basal NPK

stages.title: Vine Cuttings Planting
substeps: Propagate using 1-year-old mature vine cuttings (latchi)
Plant in a ratio of 9:1 (Female to Male plants) for cross-pollination
Plant cuttings in loops or folded in the pits, leaving ends exposed

stages.title: Vine Growth & Trellising
substeps: Provide light, frequent irrigation to establish cuttings
Erect bower (pandal) or trellis systems for vines to climb
Keep the pits completely weed-free during early establishment

stages.title: Weeding & Fertilizer
substeps: Train the vines on the bower structure using jute thread
Top dress with Nitrogen at vine branching stage
Perform regular earthing up at the base of the main vines

stages.title: Flowering & Pollination
substeps: Parval is strictly dioecious; ensure male flowers are blooming alongside females
If bee activity is low, perform manual hand pollination in the early morning
Maintain optimal moisture to prevent flower drop

stages.title: Fruit Set & Pest Control
substeps: Monitor rigorously for Fruit Fly and epilachna beetles
Use pheromone traps and poison baits to manage fruit flies
Apply fungicides if downy mildew or vine rot symptoms appear

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest fruits 15-18 days after pollination
Pick when fruits are tender, light green, and seeds inside are soft
Harvest every 3-4 days to maintain continuous yielding capability

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Deep plough the field to 20-25 cm depth
Harrow to create a very fine, loose tilth free of clods
Apply well-decomposed FYM uniformly across the field

stages.title: Seed Tuber Treatment
substeps: Select certified, disease-free seed tubers (30-40 grams)
If cutting large tubers, ensure each piece has 2-3 'eyes'
Treat tubers with Mancozeb to prevent soil-borne diseases and rot

stages.title: Planting
substeps: Plant tubers on ridges spaced 60 cm apart
Maintain plant-to-plant spacing of 20 cm
Apply basal NPK in bands slightly below the tuber level

stages.title: Vegetative Growth & Weeding
substeps: Apply pre-emergence weedicide (e.g., Metribuzin)
Irrigate immediately after planting if soil moisture is low
Maintain weed-free field until canopy closure

stages.title: Earthing Up & Top Dressing
substeps: Perform critical earthing up at 30-35 days when plants are 15-20 cm high
Top dress with remaining Nitrogen before earthing up
Earthing up covers stolons, provides space for tubers, and prevents tuber greening

stages.title: Tuber Bulking & Irrigation
substeps: Maintain consistent soil moisture; avoid dry-wet cycles to prevent tuber cracking
Monitor rigorously for Late Blight (Phytophthora infestans)
Apply prophylactic sprays of Mancozeb or Ridomil if humidity is high

stages.title: Haulm Cutting
substeps: Stop irrigation 10-15 days before harvest
Cut the aerial vegetative parts (haulms) near ground level
Leave tubers in the soil for 10 days to allow the skin to harden/cure

stages.title: Harvesting & Storage
substeps: Dig out tubers manually or use a potato digger carefully to avoid cuts
Air dry tubers in the shade for a few days to remove surface moisture
Sort, grade by size, and store in cold storage (2-4°C) for long-term preservation

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the land to a depth of 20-25 cm
Dig pits of 60x60x60 cm at a spacing of 2x2 meters
Fill pits with topsoil, well-rotted FYM, and neem cake

stages.title: Seed Sowing & Germination
substeps: Soak seeds in water for 12 hours
Treat seeds with Trichoderma viride
Sow 3-4 seeds per pit at a depth of 2-3 cm

stages.title: Vine Growth & Weeding
substeps: Thin out seedlings to retain 2 healthy plants per pit after 15 days
Perform shallow weeding around the pits
Direct growing vines along the field to ensure proper spacing

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal dose of NPK in the pits before sowing
Top dress with Nitrogen at the vine spreading stage
Apply Boron spray to reduce flower drop

stages.title: Flowering & Pollination
substeps: Monitor the appearance of male and female flowers
Ensure adequate bee population for cross-pollination
Hand pollinate in the early morning if bee activity is low

stages.title: Irrigation & Fruit Development
substeps: Irrigate pits every 3-4 days during dry weather
Provide dry grass bedding under developing fruits to prevent soil rot
Maintain consistent moisture to prevent fruit cracking

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor for Red Pumpkin Beetle and Fruit Fly
Use cue-lure traps for fruit flies
Check for powdery mildew and spray wettable sulphur if needed

stages.title: Harvesting & Storage
substeps: Harvest when the fruit stalk becomes dry and corky
Ensure the skin is hard and the color changes to orange/yellow-brown
Store harvested pumpkins in a well-ventilated, dry room to cure

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Deep plough the field to at least 25-30 cm
Harrow to create a very fine tilth free of clods
Prepare ridges to allow unhindered root growth

stages.title: Seed Sowing
substeps: Sow seeds directly on ridges at a depth of 1-1.5 cm
Maintain row spacing of 30 cm
Irrigate immediately with a light sprinkler

stages.title: Germination & Thinning
substeps: Maintain continuous surface moisture until seeds germinate
Perform critical thinning at 10-15 days after sowing
Maintain plant-to-plant spacing of 8-10 cm

stages.title: Weeding & Hoeing
substeps: Keep the field completely weed-free
Perform shallow hoeing to aerate the root zone
Earth up slightly to cover any exposed roots to prevent greening

stages.title: Fertilizer & Irrigation

Top dress with a light dose of Urea at 20 days
Provide frequent, light irrigations to ensure tender, crisp roots

stages.title: Root Development
substeps: Monitor for aphids and flea beetles
Ensure no moisture stress occurs, which causes roots to become pungent and pithy
Check root size periodically

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when roots are fully developed but still tender
Irrigate lightly before harvesting to ease uprooting
Pull roots manually by the foliage

stages.title: Post Harvest
substeps: Wash the roots thoroughly in clean water
Trim the leaves or tie them into bunches
Dispatch to the market immediately to retain crispness

stages.title: Pit Preparation

Maintain pit spacing of 1.5x2.0 meters
Mix topsoil with well-decomposed FYM and basal fertilizers

Sow 3-4 seeds per pit

stages.title: Vine Training & Trellising

Erect a bower (pandal) or trellis system
Train the growing vines to reach the trellis using jute twine

stages.title: Weeding & Fertilizer
substeps: Keep the pit area completely weed-free
Apply first top dressing of Nitrogen 30 days after sowing
Earth up the base of the vines

stages.title: Flowering & Pollination
substeps: Ridge gourd flowers typically open in the evening
Ensure adequate pollinator activity (moths/bees)
Spray Ethrel at 2-leaf and 4-leaf stages to increase female flowers

stages.title: Irrigation Management
substeps: Irrigate at 4-5 day intervals depending on soil moisture
Ensure consistent moisture during flowering and fruit setting
Avoid waterlogging to prevent vine rot

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor rigorously for Fruit Fly using cue-lure traps
Check for downy mildew on leaves during high humidity
Apply Mancozeb or Neem oil sprays as preventive measures

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest fruits while they are tender, green, and the ridges are prominent
Do not delay harvest; over-mature fruits become fibrous and spongy
Cut the fruits using a sharp knife to avoid pulling the vine

Maintain a healthy bee population in the field

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control

Set up pheromone traps for fruit flies
Spray appropriate botanical pesticides if required

stages.title: Fruit Development

Ensure fruits receive uniform sunlight for even color development
Monitor fruit size daily as they mature quickly

stages.title: Harvesting

stages.title: Trellising & Pandal Setup

Train the vines to climb using bamboo sticks and jute twine
Pinch off lateral branches until the vine reaches the pandal

stages.title: Weeding & Fertilizer

As young fruits emerge, tie a small stone (50g) to the tip of the fruit

Irrigate pits every 3-4 days in hot weather
Maintain proper drainage to prevent root rot

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control

Monitor for root-knot nematodes in the soil
Spray Mancozeb for downy mildew if symptoms appear on leaves

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest fruits when they reach full length but are still young and tender
Do not allow fruits to ripen (turn orange/red) on the vine

Cut the fruits using a sharp knife to avoid pulling the delicate vine

stages.title: Land Preparation & Ridges
substeps: Perform deep ploughing to loosen the soil to a depth of 20-25 cm
Harrow to create a fine tilth and remove all hard clods
Form strong ridges 60 cm apart to provide space for tuber expansion

stages.title: Vine Cutting Preparation
substeps: Select healthy, disease-free terminal vine cuttings (20-30 cm long)
Ensure each cutting has at least 3-4 healthy nodes
Dip the lower portion of cuttings in a mild fungicide solution

stages.title: Planting
substeps: Plant cuttings on the ridges, burying 2-3 nodes in the soil
Maintain a plant-to-plant spacing of 20-25 cm
Irrigate the field immediately after planting to ensure establishment

stages.title: Weeding & Earthing Up
substeps: Keep the field weed-free during the first 30 days
Perform hand weeding carefully to avoid disturbing the newly formed roots
Earth up the ridges to cover developing tubers and prevent weevil attacks

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal NPK with a high emphasis on Potassium (improves tuber size and quality)
Limit excess Nitrogen, as it promotes heavy foliage at the expense of tubers
Apply organic manure basally

stages.title: Vine Turning & Irrigation
substeps: Perform 'vine turning' by physically lifting vines to break roots forming at the nodes
This directs all plant energy into the main underground tubers
Irrigate every 10-15 days, maintaining moderate moisture

stages.title: Tuber Bulking & Pest Control
substeps: Ensure soil does not crack during the tuber bulking phase
Monitor strictly for the Sweet Potato Weevil (Cylas formicarius)
Fill soil cracks and use pheromone traps to manage weevils

stages.title: Harvesting & Curing
substeps: Harvest when leaves turn yellow and begin to dry
Dig carefully with a fork to avoid cutting or bruising the tubers
Cure the tubers in the sun for 3-4 days to heal wounds and improve sweetness

stages.title: Nursery Preparation
substeps: Prepare raised nursery beds or use pro-trays with coco peat
Treat seeds with Trichoderma or Captan to prevent damping-off
Sow seeds thinly, cover lightly, and protect the nursery with shade nets

stages.title: Land Prep & Transplanting
substeps: Plough the main field 3-4 times and apply a heavy dose of FYM
Prepare ridges and furrows or raised beds at 60 cm spacing
Transplant 25-30 day old robust seedlings in the late afternoon

stages.title: Weeding & Staking
substeps: Apply pre-emergence herbicide like Pendimethalin
Perform hand weeding 20-25 days after transplanting
Provide staking using bamboo sticks and twine to support indeterminate varieties

stages.title: Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply a basal dose of NPK before transplanting
Top dress with Nitrogen at 30 and 45 days after transplanting
Spray Calcium and Boron to prevent Blossom End Rot and fruit cracking

stages.title: Irrigation & Flowering
substeps: Use drip irrigation to maintain uniform moisture and prevent foliar diseases
Avoid severe moisture stress during the flowering and fruit-setting stages
Ensure adequate drainage as tomatoes are sensitive to waterlogging

stages.title: Pest & Disease Management
substeps: Monitor rigorously for Whiteflies (vector for Leaf Curl Virus) and Thrips
Scout for Tomato Fruit Borer (Helicoverpa armigera); install pheromone traps
Apply prophylactic sprays for Early and Late Blight during cloudy/humid weather

stages.title: Fruit Maturation & Harvesting
substeps: Harvest fruits based on market distance (mature green, breaker, or red ripe stage)
Pick fruits carefully by twisting the fruit away from the stem
Harvest every 3-4 days as fruits ripen continuously

stages.title: Post Harvest
substeps: Sort fruits based on color, size, and firmness
Discard any bruised, diseased, or sun-scalded fruits
Pack in ventilated plastic crates and transport to the market or processing unit

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Perform deep ploughing to loosen the soil for unhindered root growth
Harrow to create a very fine tilth free of hard clods
Prepare ridges at 30 cm spacing

stages.title: Seed Sowing
substeps: Sow seeds directly on the ridges
Maintain a sowing depth of 1-1.5 cm
Irrigate immediately with a light sprinkler or rose can

stages.title: Germination & Thinning
substeps: Maintain continuous surface moisture until complete germination
Perform crucial thinning at 10-15 days after sowing
Maintain plant-to-plant spacing of 10 cm

stages.title: Weeding & Hoeing
substeps: Keep the field completely weed-free during early growth
Perform shallow hoeing to aerate the root zone
Earth up slightly to cover any exposed roots

stages.title: Fertilizer & Irrigation
substeps: Apply basal NPK; strictly avoid fresh FYM which causes root forking
Top dress with a light dose of Urea at 20 days
Provide frequent, light irrigations to ensure tender, crisp roots

stages.title: Root Development
substeps: Monitor for aphids, flea beetles, and cabbage maggots
Ensure no moisture stress occurs, which causes roots to become pithy and woody
Check root size periodically

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when roots are 5-7 cm in diameter and still tender
Irrigate lightly before harvesting to ease uprooting
Pull roots manually, wash thoroughly, and bunch with tops attached for the market

stages.title: Land Preparation
substeps: Plough the land deeply to a depth of 20-30 cm
Prepare mounds or high ridges to allow for tuber expansion
Incorporate well-decomposed organic manure into the mounds

stages.title: Seed Tuber Preparation & Planting
substeps: Select healthy seed tubers or setts (pieces of tuber)
Treat setts with wood ash or fungicide and dry in shade for a day
Plant the setts at the top of the mounds at a depth of 10-15 cm

stages.title: Mulching & Early Growth
substeps: Cover the planted mounds with dry grass or leaves (mulching)
Mulching protects setts from excessive heat and conserves moisture
Perform gentle weeding as shoots begin to emerge

stages.title: Staking & Trellising
substeps: Erect 2-3 meter high wooden stakes or bamboo poles near the mounds
Train the growing vines to climb the stakes
Staking maximizes sunlight exposure and significantly increases yield

stages.title: Fertilizer Application & Earthing Up
substeps: Apply a basal dose of NPK fertilizers with a focus on Potassium
Top dress with Nitrogen at 1 and 2 months after planting
Perform earthing up to rebuild the mounds as they wash down with rain

stages.title: Vine Growth & Irrigation
substeps: Yam is usually rainfed but requires supplementary irrigation during dry spells
Maintain consistent soil moisture without causing waterlogging
Monitor for yam beetles and mealybugs; apply control measures if necessary

stages.title: Tuber Bulking & Maturation
substeps: Underground tubers expand rapidly during this phase
Vines will eventually begin to turn yellow and dry up, indicating maturity
Cease irrigation to allow the tubers to harden

stages.title: Harvesting & Storage
substeps: Harvest carefully using wooden spades or digging forks to avoid bruising the tubers
Clean the tubers of excess soil
Store in a well-ventilated, shaded structure (yam barn) to prevent rotting

stages.title: Pre-Monsoon Maintenance & Basin Preparation
substeps: Clean the tree basins to remove weeds and debris
Apply a heavy dose of Farm Yard Manure (FYM) around the basin
Repair the basins to harvest and retain monsoon rainwater

stages.title: Dormancy & Pruning
substeps: Perform pruning while the tree sheds its leaves (winter/early spring)
Remove dead, diseased, crisscrossing, and weak branches
Maintain a framework that allows sunlight to penetrate the canopy

stages.title: Flowering & Irrigation
substeps: Flowers appear on the new flush of shoots in spring
Provide life-saving irrigation to ensure good fruit set
Avoid severe water stress, which causes massive flower drop

stages.title: Fruit Set & Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal NPK fertilizers in the root zone after fruit set
Apply mulch in the basins to conserve moisture during hot summer months
Monitor for bark eating caterpillars and shoot borers

stages.title: Monsoon Growth & Weed Control
substeps: Rely on monsoon rains for major fruit expansion
Ensure the orchard is well-drained to prevent root rot in heavy soils
Slash orchard weeds periodically

stages.title: Fruit Bulking & Maturation
substeps: Fruits rapidly increase in size and turn from light green to a yellowish-green hue
Apply foliar sprays of zinc or boron if deficiency symptoms are observed
Protect the developing fruits from bird and monkey damage

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when fruits become hard, glossy, and translucent
Manually pluck the fruits or shake the branches over a canvas sheet
Avoid mechanical injury to the fruits during harvest

stages.title: Post Harvest & Grading
substeps: Sort fruits to remove damaged or diseased ones
Grade according to size (larger fruits fetch premium prices)
Pack in gunny bags or corrugated boxes and dispatch quickly

stages.title: Winter Dormancy & Pruning
substeps: Prune trees to maintain the central leader or desired training system
Remove water sprouts, dead wood, and diseased branches
Apply dormant oil sprays to control overwintering pests like San Jose scale

stages.title: Bud Break & Pre-Bloom
substeps: Apply basal fertilizers (NPK) into the soil
Monitor strictly for Apple Scab and apply prophylactic fungicide sprays
Ensure orchard floor is clean

stages.title: Flowering & Pollination
substeps: Introduce honeybee hives into the orchard for effective cross-pollination
Strictly prohibit the use of insecticides during the bloom period to protect bees
Protect blossoms from late spring frosts using heaters or sprinklers if necessary

stages.title: Fruit Set & Thinning
substeps: Perform chemical or manual fruit thinning to maintain optimal fruit load
Thinning ensures larger fruit size and prevents alternate bearing years
Apply foliar calcium sprays to prevent bitter pit

stages.title: Fruit Development & Irrigation
substeps: Maintain steady soil moisture using drip irrigation
Monitor and control codling moth using pheromone traps and insecticides
Manage European red mites and powdery mildew

stages.title: Color Development & Pre-Harvest
substeps: Perform summer pruning to remove shading leaves and expose fruits to sunlight
Reduce nitrogen application to allow red color development
Stop irrigation prior to harvest to improve fruit firmness and sugar content

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest based on maturity indices (starch-iodine test, firmness, ground color)
Pick fruits delicately with an upward twisting motion to keep the stem intact
Avoid bruising the fruit at all costs

stages.title: Post-Harvest & Storage
substeps: Pre-cool the apples immediately after harvest
Sort, grade by size and color, and wax the fruits if required
Store in Controlled Atmosphere (CA) cold storage for long-term supply

stages.title: Winter Dormancy & Pruning
substeps: Perform dormant pruning to shape the tree (usually an open-center system)
Thin out older fruiting wood to encourage new spur growth
Apply Bordeaux mixture spray to prevent bacterial canker

stages.title: Bud Swell & Flowering
substeps: Apricots bloom early and are highly susceptible to spring frosts
Implement frost protection measures (orchard heaters/wind machines) if required
Apply fungicides to control Blossom Blight (Monilinia)

stages.title: Fruit Set & Thinning
substeps: Thin heavy fruit sets early to prevent branches from breaking under weight
Thinning also ensures larger, high-quality fruits
Apply basal NPK fertilizers

stages.title: Fruit Development & Irrigation
substeps: Provide regular irrigation during the rapid fruit growth phase
Apricots are sensitive to waterlogging, so ensure excellent drainage
Monitor for aphids, scale insects, and peach twig borer

stages.title: Maturation & Color Change
substeps: Reduce irrigation as fruits begin to change color from green to yellow/orange
Monitor rigorously for fruit fly infestations
Place bait sprays or traps to manage pests before harvest

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvesting is done in 2-3 pickings as fruits mature unevenly
For fresh market, pick when firm but colored; for drying, pick when fully ripe
Handle very delicately as apricot skin bruises easily

stages.title: Post-Harvest Processing
substeps: Sort and grade the harvested fruits
For dry apricots: pit the fruits, treat with sulfur smoke to retain color, and sun-dry
Pack fresh fruits in soft, padded trays and keep in cold storage

stages.title: Autumn Maintenance
substeps: Apply well-rotted FYM in the tree basins
Clean the orchard floor of fallen leaves and mummified fruits to reduce disease carryover
Prepare the orchard for winter dormancy

stages.title: Land Preparation & Pit Digging
substeps: Deep plough the field and level it
Dig pits measuring 60x60x60 cm at a spacing of 1.8x1.8 meters
Fill pits with topsoil mixed with 10 kg FYM, neem cake, and super phosphate

stages.title: Sucker Treatment & Planting
substeps: Select healthy sword suckers (1.5 - 2.0 kg) or tissue culture plantlets
Pare the roots and dip suckers in a nematicide/fungicide slurry (e.g., Carbendazim)
Plant the suckers in the center of the pits and press the soil firmly

stages.title: Early Vegetative Growth & Weeding
substeps: Irrigate immediately after planting and regularly thereafter
Keep the field completely weed-free during the initial establishment
Apply pre-emergence herbicides if necessary

stages.title: Fertilizer Application & Earthing Up
substeps: Apply Nitrogen and Potassium in 3-4 split doses during the 3rd, 5th, and 7th months
Perform earthing up (mounding soil around the base) to prevent the plant from toppling
Banana is a heavy feeder requiring high Potassium for bunch development

stages.title: Desuckering & Maintenance
substeps: Regularly remove side suckers (desuckering) to direct energy to the main mother plant
Retain only one follower sucker towards the end of the crop cycle (ratooning)
Remove dried, diseased, or decaying leaves

stages.title: Shooting (Flowering) & Irrigation
substeps: The inflorescence (bunch) emerges from the heart of the pseudostem
Provide ample irrigation as water stress at this stage severely affects bunch size
Cut the terminal male flower bud (denavelling) once fruit setting is complete

stages.title: Bunch Development & Propping
substeps: Tie bamboo poles (propping) to the pseudostem to support the heavy bunch from wind
Cover the bunch with perforated polythene bags (skirting) to protect against thrips and sunburn
Spray Potassium sulfate to improve fruit size and quality

stages.title: Harvesting & Post Harvest
substeps: Harvest when fruits become plump, angles become rounded, and top leaves start drying
Cut the bunch carefully; chop the pseudostem, leaving a stump
De-hand the bunch, wash to remove latex, treat with fungicide, and artificially ripen using ethylene gas

stages.title: Summer Pruning & Basin Preparation
substeps: Perform heavy pruning during the hot summer (May-June) when the tree is dormant
Remove dead, diseased, and crisscrossing branches to build a strong framework
Clean the tree basins and incorporate Farm Yard Manure (FYM)

stages.title: Dormancy Breaking & New Flush
substeps: Apply basal NPK fertilizers before the onset of the monsoon
New vegetative shoots emerge from the pruned branches
Keep the basins weed-free to prevent competition for nutrients

stages.title: Vegetative Growth & Canopy Management
substeps: Allow the new canopy to develop vigorously with monsoon rains
Maintain proper drainage to prevent root rot in heavy soils
Monitor for early defoliating insects and apply preventive botanical sprays

stages.title: Flowering & Pollination
substeps: Flowering typically occurs from August to September on the new growth
Ensure adequate bee activity for cross-pollination to improve fruit set
Avoid pesticide sprays during peak flowering to protect pollinators

stages.title: Fruit Set & Early Development
substeps: Provide life-saving irrigation post-monsoon to prevent fruit drop
Apply a top dressing of Nitrogen and Potassium
Spray micronutrients like Zinc and Boron to enhance fruit retention

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor rigorously for the Ber Fruit Fly (Carpomyia vesuviana)
Destroy fallen infested fruits and use bait sprays
Apply wettable sulphur to control powdery mildew during cool nights

stages.title: Fruit Bulking & Maturation
substeps: Maintain consistent soil moisture for maximum fruit size expansion
Protect the orchard from bird damage as fruits start turning color
Observe color change from green to light yellow/reddish-brown

stages.title: Harvesting & Post Harvest
substeps: Harvest fruits manually in 3-4 pickings as they mature unevenly
Pick when fruits attain proper color and are crisp but not overripe
Sort, grade by size, and pack in ventilated corrugated boxes

stages.title: Pre-Monsoon Maintenance & Manuring
substeps: Clean the orchard floor and widen the tree basins
Apply a heavy dose of well-rotted FYM, Neem cake, and bone meal
Repair irrigation channels or drip lines before the rains begin

stages.title: Vegetative Flush & Canopy Management
substeps: Sapota is evergreen; prune only to remove dead wood or interlacing branches
Allow the new vegetative flush to develop with the onset of the monsoon
Ensure the orchard does not suffer from waterlogging

stages.title: Flowering & Pollination
substeps: Sapota flowers continuously, but major flushes occur twice a year
Maintain moderate soil moisture to prevent flower drop
Rely on wind and insects for pollination

stages.title: Fruit Set & Fertilizer Application
substeps: Apply basal NPK fertilizers after successful fruit set
Sapota is a heavy feeder of Potassium; ensure adequate supply for fruit size
Apply organic mulches to conserve moisture

stages.title: Irrigation Management
substeps: Provide regular irrigation during the dry spells (every 10-15 days)
Use drip irrigation to maintain uniform moisture levels
Moisture stress leads to heavy flower and immature fruit drop

stages.title: Pest & Disease Control
substeps: Monitor strictly for the Sapota Seed Borer and Chiku Moth (leaf webber)
Remove and destroy webbed shoots and infested fruits
Apply prophylactic sprays of appropriate systemic insecticides if required

stages.title: Fruit Maturation
substeps: Fruits take a long time (4-6 months) to mature from set to harvest
The brown scurf on the fruit surface starts falling off
Scratch the fruit surface; if the tissue below is yellow (not green), it is mature

stages.title: Harvesting & Ripening
substeps: Harvest fruits manually using a harvester with a net to prevent falling and bruising
Wash fruits to remove latex from the stalk end
Artificially ripen using ethylene gas in a ripening chamber before retail sale

stages.title: Leaf Fall & Summer Dormancy
substeps: The tree undergoes natural leaf fall and enters dormancy during extreme summer
Withhold irrigation to enforce dormancy and allow the tree to rest
Clean the orchard floor of all fallen leaves

stages.title: Pruning & Basin Preparation
substeps: Perform light pruning just before the monsoon to encourage new shoots
Remove dried, diseased, and overcrowding twigs
Prepare basins and apply FYM along with basal NPK fertilizers

stages.title: Bud Break & Flowering
substeps: New leaves and flowers emerge simultaneously with early monsoon showers
Ensure adequate soil moisture to prevent flower desiccation
Maintain a weed-free environment around the tree basins

stages.title: Pollination & Fruit Set
substeps: Natural pollination is often poor due to dichogamy (stamens and pistils mature at different times)
Perform manual hand pollination using a brush to dramatically increase fruit set and symmetry
Maintain high humidity around the canopy

stages.title: Fruit Development & Moisture Conservation
substeps: Apply a top dressing of Potassium to improve fruit size and sweetness
Mulch the basins with dry grass to retain soil moisture
Provide life-saving irrigation if there is a prolonged break in the monsoon

stages.title: Pest Management (Mealybugs)
substeps: Monitor rigorously for Mealybugs, the most devastating pest of custard apple
Apply sticky bands on the tree trunk to prevent ants from carrying mealybugs
Spray Neem oil or appropriate insecticides targeting the fruit crevices

stages.title: Maturation & Color Change
substeps: Fruits bulk up and the areoles (segments on the skin) become prominent
Maturity is indicated when the inter-areolar space widens and turns pale yellow
Stop irrigation prior to harvest to prevent fruit splitting

stages.title: Harvesting & Packaging
substeps: Harvest fruits individually while they are firm but fully mature
Clip the stalk carefully; do not pull, as the skin is delicate
Pack in single-layer cushioned boxes to prevent bruising during transport

stages.title: Dormancy & Pruning
substeps: Perform pruning during the dormant season (winter/summer depending on the region)
Figs bear fruit on new wood; head back previous season's growth to stimulate new shoots
Apply Bordeaux paste to cut ends to prevent fungal infections

stages.title: Sprouting & Basin Manuring
substeps: Apply heavy organic manures (FYM, poultry manure) and basal NPK
Irrigate the field to stimulate bud break and uniform sprouting
Maintain completely weed-free basins

stages.title: Vegetative Growth & Canopy Training
substeps: Train the new shoots to maintain an open center for maximum sunlight penetration
Remove excess water sprouts from the base of the trunk
Apply a light top dressing of Nitrogen to boost leaf expansion

stages.title: Syconium (Fig) Formation & Irrigation
substeps: Tiny figs (syconia) appear in the axils of the leaves on the new shoots
Provide regular, moderate irrigation; figs are highly sensitive to both drought and waterlogging
Apply Potassium foliar sprays to aid fruit development

stages.title: Pest & Disease Management
substeps: Monitor strictly for Fig Rust, which causes premature defoliation
Apply prophylactic fungicide sprays (e.g., Chlorothalonil) before rust establishes
Check the trunk for stem borers and apply localized treatments

stages.title: Fruit Swelling & Maturation
substeps: Fruits undergo rapid swelling, and the 'eye' (ostiole) at the bottom begins to open slightly
Protect the orchard from birds using nets or bird scarers
Reduce irrigation as fruits change color to prevent splitting and watery taste

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvesting requires multiple pickings over several weeks as figs ripen sequentially
Pick fruits when they are soft to the touch and the neck slightly bends
Harvest with the stalk attached to prevent latex exudation and spoilage

stages.title: Post-Harvest Processing
substeps: Figs are highly perishable; dispatch to the fresh market immediately in cushioned trays
For dried figs, treat with sulfur fumes to maintain color and prevent mold
Sun-dry or dehydrate the fruits until moisture is reduced to 15-20%

stages.title: Foundation Pruning (Back Pruning) & Rest
substeps: Perform heavy foundation pruning in April (post-harvest) leaving 1-2 buds
Apply heavy basal organic manures and NPK in the root zone
Allow the vines to rest and recover from the previous harvest

stages.title: Cane Development & Vegetative Growth
substeps: New vegetative shoots emerge; tie them to the trellis wires (bower/Y-trellis)
Perform sub-cane pruning to increase fruitful bud differentiation
Monitor and control downy mildew during the monsoon rains

stages.title: Fruit Pruning (Forward Pruning) & Dormancy Break
substeps: Perform forward pruning in October/November leaving 5-7 buds on mature canes
Apply Hydrogen Cyanamide (Dormex) paste on buds to ensure uniform sprouting
Withhold irrigation prior to pruning to force the vine into a brief stress period

stages.title: Pre-Bloom & Flowering
substeps: New shoots emerge with flower clusters (inflorescences)
Apply prophylactic sprays for Powdery Mildew and Thrips
Pinch the shoot tips to direct energy toward the developing flower clusters

stages.title: Berry Set & GA3 Application
substeps: Berries set after flowering; maintain optimal soil moisture
Dip bunches in Gibberellic Acid (GA3) solution at 3-4 mm berry size for elongation
Perform manual bunch thinning to remove excess or misshapen berries

stages.title: Berry Development & Canopy Management
substeps: Apply Potassium and Calcium through drip irrigation to strengthen berry skin
Remove basal leaves around the bunches to improve aeration and sunlight penetration
Monitor for Mealybugs and apply systemic insecticides

stages.title: Veraison (Color Change) & Maturation
substeps: Berries become translucent and start changing color (Veraison)
Stop Nitrogen application completely; focus entirely on Potassium
Check Total Soluble Solids (TSS/Brix) regularly to monitor sugar accumulation

stages.title: Harvesting & Grading
substeps: Harvest bunches when they reach the desired Brix level (usually 18-22° Brix)
Pick during the cool morning hours and immediately transfer to a packhouse
Grade bunches, pack in corrugated boxes with grape guards (SO2 pads), and pre-cool

stages.title: Pruning & Canopy Management
substeps: Perform pruning to regulate the crop (Bahar treatment) and maintain canopy
Remove crisscrossing, dead, and diseased branches to ensure sunlight penetration
Head back terminal shoots to encourage lateral branching

stages.title: Basin Preparation & Manuring
substeps: Clean the tree basins and remove weeds
Apply a heavy dose of Farm Yard Manure (FYM) mixed with neem cake
Apply basal NPK fertilizers in the root zone

stages.title: Vegetative Growth & Flush
substeps: New vegetative shoots emerge following pruning and fertilization
Maintain optimal soil moisture to support the new flush
Monitor for early pest activity like aphids and whiteflies

stages.title: Flowering
substeps: Flowers appear in the axils of the new leaves
Ensure adequate bee population for pollination
Avoid pesticide sprays during peak flowering hours

stages.title: Fruit Set & Early Growth
substeps: Provide life-saving irrigation to prevent fruit drop
Apply a top dressing of Nitrogen and Potassium
Spray micronutrients like Zinc and Boron if deficiency is observed

stages.title: Fruit Development & Irrigation
substeps: Ensure consistent soil moisture for maximum fruit expansion
Avoid severe water stress followed by heavy watering to prevent fruit cracking
Bag individual fruits if necessary to protect from fruit flies and birds

stages.title: Pest & Disease Management
substeps: Monitor rigorously for the Guava Fruit Fly (Bactrocera dorsalis) using pheromone traps
Check for mealybugs and apply sticky bands to the trunk
Apply appropriate fungicides to control Guava Wilt and Anthracnose

stages.title: Harvesting & Post Harvest
substeps: Harvest fruits when they change color from dark green to light yellowish-green
Pick fruits manually to avoid bruising
Sort, grade, and pack in corrugated boxes with cushioning for transport

stages.title: Pre-Monsoon Maintenance & Basin Prep
substeps: Clear the orchard floor of dried leaves and debris
Widen the tree basins to capture monsoon rainwater
Remove any parasitic plants or epiphytes from the trunk and branches

stages.title: Manuring
substeps: Apply well-rotted FYM, compost, and basal NPK to the basins
Jackfruit responds well to organic manuring
Cover the applied manure with a thin layer of soil and mulch

stages.title: Vegetative Flush
substeps: Allow the tree to produce a new flush of leaves
Ensure adequate drainage during heavy monsoon rains to prevent root rot
Minimal pruning is required, mostly removing dead wood

stages.title: Flowering & Spike Emergence
substeps: Male and female flower spikes (inflorescences) emerge on the trunk and main branches (cauliflory)
Maintain soil moisture to prevent flower drop
Observe the distinct appearance of female spikes which are plumper

stages.title: Fruit Set & Pollination
substeps: Pollination is mainly carried out by wind and small insects
Remove excess or malformed fruit spikes to allow proper development of remaining fruits
Apply a light top dressing of Potassium

stages.title: Fruit Development & Bulking
substeps: Fruits undergo massive expansion; jackfruit is the largest tree-borne fruit
Provide life-saving irrigation during dry spells to ensure maximum fruit size
Monitor for fruit rot diseases during humid weather

stages.title: Pest Control & Bagging
substeps: Monitor for the Jackfruit Borer (shoot and fruit borer)
Cover developing fruits with gunny bags or specialized fruit bags to prevent borer attacks
Remove and destroy any infested or rotting fruits immediately

stages.title: Harvesting
substeps: Harvest when the fruit yields a dull, hollow sound when tapped
The spines on the fruit surface become well-spaced and yield to moderate pressure
Cut the thick stalk carefully and lower the heavy fruit using ropes to avoid damage

stages.title: Post-Harvest Pruning & Cleaning
substeps: Perform light pruning to remove dry, diseased, and crisscrossing branches
Clean the tree basins and remove fallen fruits/seeds to prevent fungal buildup
Maintain the tree framework for the next season

stages.title: Dormancy & Basin Preparation
substeps: The tree enters a brief resting phase during the winter
Prepare basins around the trunk
Keep the orchard weed-free

stages.title: Manuring & Irrigation
substeps: Apply well-rotted FYM and a basal dose of NPK
Irrigate the tree basins to initiate the spring flush
Apply mulch to conserve moisture

stages.title: New Flush & Pre-Flowering
substeps: New vegetative shoots and leaves emerge
Ensure consistent soil moisture
Monitor for leaf-eating caterpillars and apply botanical pesticides if needed

stages.title: Flowering & Pollination
substeps: Small, fragrant white flowers appear in clusters
Pollination is highly dependent on honeybees and flies
Do not apply any chemical sprays during this period to protect pollinators

stages.title: Fruit Set & Development
substeps: Fruits set and begin their initial green growth phase
Provide regular irrigation during the hot summer months to prevent fruit drop
Apply a light dose of Potassium to aid fruit bulking

stages.title: Color Change & Maturation
substeps: Fruits transition from green to pink/crimson, and finally to deep purple/black
Protect the orchard from birds and bats using nets or acoustic deterrents
Monitor for fruit fly damage

stages.title: Harvesting & Grading
substeps: Harvest fruits manually by picking or shaking branches over a net/canvas
Harvesting must be done daily as fruits ripen unevenly and drop quickly
Sort, grade, and pack immediately in small bamboo baskets or perforated boxes due to high perishability

stages.title: Pruning & Dead Wood Removal
substeps: Prune immediately after harvest (Jan-Feb) to remove dead and diseased wood
Remove water sprouts and interlocking branches to ensure proper aeration
